title: elaeocarpus roslii
intro: Elaeocarpus roslii, also known as the Fraser's Mallet or Runggus tree, is a species of flowering plant in the Elaeocarpaceae family native to Borneo. The medicinal properties of Elaeocarpus roslii are attributed to its leaves, which contain various compounds including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids that exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus roslii include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a traditional remedy for wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Elaeocarpus roslii include infusions of its leaves, which can be consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to the lack of scientific studies on its safety and efficacy.
